site.title: ASHWATH.SYS — Backend Engineer & System Design Enthusiast
site.description: Ashwath Soni builds the parts of the internet you're not supposed to see. Distributed systems, cloud infrastructure, and AI agents — served from Punjab, India.

statusQuips: have you tried turning the Docker container off and on?
the cat pushed to prod again (wasn't me, I swear)
just one more refactor, I swear
my AI agent wrote this quip
hobbies? never heard of that npm package
currently arguing with a RAG pipeline
touching grass is just a mock API call away
localhost:3000 is my actual home address
powered by tea and unhandled promise rejections
deploying to prod on a Friday... what could go wrong?
I'd have a hobby if it had a REST API
hallucinating slightly less than the LLMs I use
eventual consistency means 'it'll work eventually, right?'
*sips tea* — ah, the taste of productivity

titles.text: I suck at making nice UI/UX
I am bad at math (like really bad)

profile.name: Ashwath Soni
profile.tagline: I build the parts of the internet you're not supposed to see.
profile.status: Final-year B.Tech CSE @ Guru Nanak Dev University (2023–2027)
profile.bio: Chronically online, terminally curious. I have exactly zero hobbies — no movies, no music, no touching grass. Just distributed systems, cloud infrastructure, and AI agents doing my bidding. My idea of a good time is watching a queue drain at 3 AM. Everything I ship is built with a production-first mindset: real SaaS, real users, real pager anxiety.

contact.telemetry: contact.gateway — POST /api/v1/contact · auth: none · rate limit: be reasonable
contact.title: Send a Request

experience.description: Engineering automation workflows and scalable systems that real companies bet real money on.
highlights: Built the core Elysia API layer for a **RAG-as-a-Service** platform: **multi-tenant architecture**, billing, invite workflows, and knowledge-base-linked chat systems.

Engineered a corporate WhatsApp learning bot upskilling 3,000+ frontline workers — stateful training engine, AWS Rekognition selfie verification, automated assessment lockouts, OTP-based certificates.
Developed the backend for an EdTech live-session module used by top Indian institutes (IITs/IIMs): faculty onboarding, automatic scheduling, automated invoicing, systematized email pipelines.

experience.description: Wearing the architect hat and the team-lead hat at the same time. Both fit.
highlights: Architected and deployed a production-grade e-commerce backend on a custom VPS: Docker, PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS S3.
Led direct client communications and managed a small backend team — enforcing code quality and shipping features that stay shipped.

experience.description: The foundational engineer for multiple commercial clients — cloud accounts, database schemas, and CI/CD, all from an empty repo.
highlights: Developed a live insurance operations platform (NDA): **OCR/Gemini AI** policy ingestion cut manual entry time by 90%.
Built an automated reconciliation system processing 500+ records in under 3 seconds.
Implemented a patient appointment system with Next.js server actions and EmailJS.

projects.title: CodeSentinel
projects.tagline: The cyber-defense agent that never sleeps. (Neither do I, but that's beside the point.)
projects.description: An **autonomous, privacy-first** security agent built to democratize cybersecurity. It reads intent, context, and patterns instead of just grepping for CVEs — a force multiplier for teams that can't afford a red team.
highlights: Static mode: audits local repos, auto-fixes vulnerabilities, and packages the changes into ready-to-merge GitHub PRs.
Dynamic mode: explores live URLs like a security researcher — uncovered 15+ hidden endpoints (including exposed Supabase REST/storage URLs) on an authorized target.
Reached **plaintext credentials** on a live authorized target in under 10 minutes.

projects.title: InsureZeal
projects.tagline: Insurance paperwork, minus the humans who hate doing it.
projects.description: A **fully automated** insurance management SaaS covering the entire policy lifecycle: creation, claims workflows, customer portal, analytics, document processing, and multi-channel comms — all gated behind **granular role-based access control**.
highlights: OCR + Gemini AI policy ingestion cut manual data entry time by 90%.
Automated reconciliation engine chews through 500+ records in under 3 seconds.
Granular RBAC so the intern can't nuke the claims table.

projects.title: Saaz Creations
projects.tagline: A real store, making real money, on a VPS I babysit personally.
projects.description: A **live e-commerce platform** with smooth browsing and checkout, WhatsApp marketing, Fast2SMS broadcasting, Razorpay payment workflows, and automated campaign management. Deployed on a **custom VPS** I run like a tiny data center.
highlights: Production-grade backend on self-managed infrastructure: Docker, PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS S3.
Integrated WhatsApp + SMS campaign automation that actually converts.
Razorpay checkout flows hardened against every weird payment state I could produce.

projects.title: Kmaxx Hospital
projects.tagline: Hospital management with a CMS the staff actually use.
projects.description: A comprehensive hospital management and showcase platform with department management, doctor profiles, and a fully integrated appointment booking system that fires off email notifications automatically.
highlights: Custom-built Sanity CMS workflows for non-technical staff.
Appointment booking with automatic email notifications to doctors and patients.

projects.title: Mundra Dental Clinic
projects.tagline: Proof that I can, in fact, make things look nice.
projects.description: A dedicated frontend showcase for a dental clinic — services, treatment specializations, and a highly responsive design optimized for patients browsing at 11 PM with a toothache.
highlights: Fully responsive patient-first design.
Service and treatment discovery tuned for conversion.

projects.title: Google Maps Scraping Extension
projects.tagline: Location intelligence for people who'd rather not click 400 times.
projects.description: A browser extension that efficiently extracts and processes business data from Google Maps — streamlined lead generation and location intelligence without the manual grind.
highlights: In-browser extraction pipeline with structured data output.
Built for lead-gen workflows: scrape, clean, export, done.

achievements.title: GDGoC Lead & CESS Tech Club Co-Lead
achievements.detail: Lead Organizer for Google Developer Groups on Campus at GNDU. Spearheaded 2 major offline hackathons (150+ participants each) and 4 online hackathons.

achievements.title: 2nd Prize — StatusBrew Hackathon 1.0 & 2.0
achievements.detail: Back-to-back podium finishes. Same hackathon, twice the silverware.

achievements.title: Ranked 7th of 70+ teams — NIT Jalandhar National Hackathon
Backend / Team Lead across 20+ hackathons
achievements.detail: Task planning, code reviews, and keeping the demo from crashing at minute 59.

activity: kmaxx-mailer: dispatched 12 doctor notifications. zero bounces.

webhook: InsureZeal reconciled 500 rows in 1.9s
gc: collected 3 zombie promises
bun: installed 1,402 packages in 0.4s. speed is art.

cat.service: requested pets (priority: high)
rekognition: verified 1 selfie, 0 impostors
queue drained. serotonin +5
merged PR #418: 'i swear this is the last refactor'

boot.granted: > ACCESS GRANTED — welcome to the system.

contact.responseMessage: Request accepted. Response SLA: faster than my CI pipeline.

game.intro: Oh — you found one! My bad I drink Sprite way too often and keep leaving cans all over my website. Can you help me find them all?
lines: Can #{n} recovered! The recycling gods smile upon you.
That's #{n}. My mom is thanking you already.
#{n} down. This one was behind a deserted project.
#{n}! You're better at this than my garbage collector.
Halfway-ish. #{n} cans. The cats are watching, impressed.
#{n}! Somewhere a vending machine feels seen.
#{n} cans. Your git blame is spotless.
Almost there — #{n}. One more sip… I mean, can.
#{n} of 10. Sprite-free and victorious. Now find the tea cups (just kidding).
game.reward: ACHIEVEMENT UNLOCKED: Certified Snack Locator 🥤 — you found all 10 sprite cans. Your reward is this deep sense of accomplishment (and the knowledge that the cats drank the rest). A real prize is loading… ETA: never. Thanks for cleaning up after me.

footer.note: // there are 10 types of people: those who read this footer, and those who don't.
footer.copyright: all puns and facts handcrafted, no cats were rate-limited in the making of this site.
footer.stackLine: built with love · one editable JSON file · Next.js & Bun · far too much tokens spent on Claude · deployed on a VPS I babysit personally
